The rare<ref name="CSWE-Douglas III">{{CSWECite|I|202|Douglas III}}</ref> and elusive<ref name="Gamer">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}</ref> '''jackelope''' was a small horned creature native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Douglas III]],<ref name="CSWE-Jackelope" /> a part of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] region. Famous game [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Mendel Douglas]], after whom Douglas III was named, traversed the planet in a bid to acquire the jackelope's hide. Although the jackelope made it back for his collection, Douglas [[Death/Legends|did not survive]] the expedition.<ref name="Gamer" />

By [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> the working-class [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] [[Poly Pyramid]] on the neighboring Centrality planet [[Rafa IV]] decorated its interior with incompetently [[Taxidermist|taxidermized]] [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] fauna, both nailed to the walls and suspended from the ceiling. A jackelope from Douglas III numbered among the creatures displayed.<ref name="Mindharp">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The jackelope was introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Canon|lore]] by author [[L. Neil Smith]] in the [[1983]] novel ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'',<ref name="Mindharp" /> the first volume in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] series.<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion" />

In North American folklore, the [[Wikipedia:Jackalope|jackalope]] is a legendary creature with the body of a [[Wikipedia:Hare|jackrabbit]] and the antlers of a [[Wikipedia:Pronghorn|pronghorn antelope]]. Twentieth-century [[Wikipedia:Taxidermy|taxidermist]] Douglas Herrick is credited with creating the American jackalope, which developed into a tourist attraction in Herrick's hometown of [[Wikipedia:Douglas, Wyoming|Douglas, Wyoming]], where jackalope hunting licenses are sold.<ref name="NY Times">{{WebCite|url=http://www.nytimes.com/2003/01/19/us/douglas-herrick-82-dies-father-of-west-s-jackalope.html|text=Douglas Herrick, 82, Dies; Father of West's Jackalope|author=Martin, Douglas|date=2003-01-19|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|The New York Times]]|archivedate=20170916034716}}</ref>
